原文:

 

The key is running only 1 worker when start uvicorn

Content

For local Python app development, use a Docker container for non-application services like databases, Redis, etc. If all of your services are running in containers, you SHOULD use remote debug.

If all of your services are run in container you SHOULD use remote debug

0. Dockerfile

Here, I will use docker-compose as example

version: "3.8"
services: pgsql: image: postgres:13 restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD=trust - POSTGRES_USER=root - POSTGRES_PASSWORD= - POSTGRES_DB=postgres volumes: - postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data ports: - '5432:5432' privileged: true
volumes: postgres:

1. Setup Run/Debug configurations

It's located in the top-right corner.

2. Setup local running configuration

Choose "FastAPI" for the running configuration.

remember to set --workers=1

3. Setup container running configuration

Choose Docker Compose for your configuration.

4. Add local configuration

Under "Before launch" settings, add your local running configuration.

Complete setup

5. Start debugger as a happy debugger

Once suspended, whchi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, whchi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by whchi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If whchi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag whchi:

Make all posts by whchi less visible

whchi consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging whchi will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: whchi

item.title: 使用 Python 抓取谷歌学术搜索 | Scrape Google Scholar Using Python
item.description:
对于特别希望访问互联网上可用的高质量研究内容的企业来说，谷歌学术数据可能是一个很好的选择。

在本教程中，我们将学习使用 Python 和库请求以及 BeautifulSoup 来抓取 Google 学术搜索结果。

为什么要抓取谷歌学术搜索？

抓取 Google Scholar 可以为您提供各种好处：

内容访问——通过谷歌学术搜索，您可以访问由世界各地经验丰富的科学家发布的基于研究的内容。

分析趋势——开发人员可以通过从 Google Scholar Results 中提取文章标题、摘要和引用次数等信息来分析学术文献中的趋势

让我们开始使用 Python 抓取 Google Scholar

在本节中，我们将准备一个使用 Python 来抓取 Google Scholar Results 的基本脚本，但首先让我们完成该项目的要求。

设置

如果您尚未在设备上安装 Python，请观看以下视频：

  1. 如何在 Windows 上安装 Python？

  2. 如何在 MacOS 上安装 Python？

或者您可以直接从他们的官方网站安装 Python。

要求：

为了抓取谷歌学术搜索结果，我们将使用这两个 Python 库：

您可以在项目终端中运行以下命令来安装库。

 pip install requests pip install beautifulsoup4

抓取谷歌学术搜索结果

在本节中，我们将从谷歌学术搜索中抓取有机结果。

在您的桌面上打开此链接。 我们将从这些结果中抓取标题、链接、ID、显示链接、摘要和站点链接。

以下是抓取 Google Organic Sc​​holar Results 的完整代码：

 import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def getScholarData(): try: url = "https://www.google.com/scholar?q=Quantum+Physics&hl=en" headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_11_3) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } response = requests.get(url, headers=headers) soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser') scholar_results = [] for el in soup.select(".gs_ri"): scholar_results.append({ "title": el.select(".gs_rt")[0].text, "title_link": el.select(".gs_rt a")[0]["href"], "id": el.select(".gs_rt a")[0]["id"], "displayed_link": el.select(".gs_a")[0].text, "snippet": el.select(".gs_rs")[0].text.replace("\n", ""), "cited_by_count": el.select(".gs_nph+ a")[0].text, "cited_link": "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select(".gs_nph+ a")[0]["href"], "versions_count": el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0].text, "versions_link": "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0]["href"] if el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0].text else "", }) for i in range(len(scholar_results)): scholar_results[i] = {key: value for key, value in scholar_results[i].items() if value != "" and value is not None} print(scholar_results) except Exception as e: print(e) getScholarData()

您可以使用此CSS 选择器小工具从 HTML 结果中找到您想要的各个元素的标签。

这将从网页返回以下数据：

 [ { 'title': '[BOOK][B] Quantum physics', 'title_link': 'https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=qFtQiVmjWUEC&oi=fnd&pg=PA28&dq=Quantum+Physics&ots=tuFPLNEOmu&sig=tRYUJyK8VECC_j1Lwpbl6bjC5ag', 'id': 'cU32d0ZoSA0J', 'displayed_link': 'S Gasiorowicz - 2007 - books.google.com', 'snippet': '… the Old Quantum Theory. This material appears in every textbook on modern physics in one … the creation of quantum mechanics, and it highlights the differences between classical and …', 'cited_by_count': 'Cited by 1400', 'cited_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cites=957129572685860209&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en', 'versions_count': 'All 10 versions', 'versions_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cluster=957129572685860209&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5' }, { 'title': '[BOOK][B] Quantum physics', 'title_link': 'https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=hBlZu4M51IMC&oi=fnd&pg=PA1&dq=Quantum+Physics&ots=uEsu7Kytk-&sig=54fJyjI177Zm2gC7HZcgM_iCEJU', 'id': 'ENaNHF5MoQsJ', 'displayed_link': 'M Le Bellac - 2011 - books.google.com', 'snippet': '… allow the fundamentals of quantum mechanics to be tested … in quantum physics. It will also allow us to give a small sample of the current ideas on the notion of measurement in quantum …', 'cited_by_count': 'Cited by 211', 'cited_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cites=838034972757317136&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en', 'versions_count': 'All 4 versions', 'versions_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cluster=838034972757317136&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5' }, ....

抓取谷歌学术引用结果

然后，我们将使用从抓取有机结果中获得的ID来抓取引用结果。

 import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def getData(): try: url = "https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com&output=cite" headers = {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/104.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"} response = requests.get(url, headers=headers) soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, "html.parser") cite_results = [] for el in soup.select("#gs_citt tr"): cite_results.append({ "title": el.select_one(".gs_cith").text.strip(), "snippet": el.select_one(".gs_citr").text.strip() }) links = [] for el in soup.select("#gs_citi .gs_citi"): links.append({ "name": el.text.strip(), "link": el.get("href") }) print(cite_results) print(links) except Exception as e: print(e) getData()

如果你看一下 URL，在信息之后我们使用了从上一节中获得的第一个有机结果的 ID。

我们的结果应该是这样的：

 [ { 'title': 'MLA', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, Stephen. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons, 2007.' }, { 'title': 'APA', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, S. (2007). Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons.' }, { 'title': 'Chicago', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, Stephen. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons, 2007.' }, { 'title': 'Harvard', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, S., 2007. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons.' }, { 'title': 'Vancouver', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz S. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons; 2007 Jan 29.' } ] [ { 'name': 'BibTeX', 'link': 'https: //scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.bib?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=4&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' }, { 'name': 'EndNote', 'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.enw?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=3&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' }, { 'name': 'RefMan', 'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.ris?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=2&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' }, { 'name': 'RefWorks', 'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.rfw?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=1&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' } ]

抓取谷歌学术作者

现在，让我们抓取已发表有关量子物理学的优质内容的作者简介。

这是我们的代码：

 import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def getScholarProfiles(): try: url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&view_op=search_authors&mauthors=Quantum+Physics" headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.54 Safari/537.36" } response = requests.get(url, headers=headers) soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser') scholar_profiles = [] for el in soup.select('.gsc_1usr'): profile = { 'name': el.select_one('.gs_ai_name').get_text(), 'name_link': 'https://scholar.google.com' + el.select_one('.gs_ai_name a')['href'], 'position': el.select_one('.gs_ai_aff').get_text(), 'email': el.select_one('.gs_ai_eml').get_text(), 'departments': el.select_one('.gs_ai_int').get_text(), 'cited_by_count': el.select_one('.gs_ai_cby').get_text().split(' ')[2] } scholar_profiles.append({k: v for k, v in profile.items() if v}) print(scholar_profiles) except Exception as e: print(e) getScholarProfiles()

我们的结果应该是这样的：

 [ { name: 'Georg Kresse', name_link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ', position: 'University of Vienna, Faculty of Physics, Professor for Computational Quantum Mechanics', email: 'Verified email at univie.ac.at', departments: 'density functional theory first principles calculations many body theory condensed matter physics materials science ', cited_by_count: '345869' }, { name: 'Manuel Proissl', name_link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=ikHSFIkAAAAJ', position: 'Data Science Leader, Quantum Computing Technologist, Physicist', email: 'Verified email at accenture.com', departments: 'quantum computing machine learning deep learning causal inference ', cited_by_count: '82357' }, ....

抓取谷歌学术作者简介

让我们不要创建一个从 Google Author Profile中提取数据的抓取工具。
首先，我们将提取有关作者的一些主要详细信息，然后我们将转到其发布的内容部分。

 import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def getAuthorProfileData(): try: url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ" headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_13_5) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } response = requests.get(url, headers=headers) print(response.status_code) soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser') author_results = {} author_results['name'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_in").get_text() author_results['position'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_inw+ .gsc_prf_il").text author_results['email'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_ivh").text author_results['published_content'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_int").text print(author_results) except Exception as e: print(e) getAuthorProfileData()

我们的结果应该是这样的：

 { name: 'Georg Kresse', position: 'University of Vienna, Faculty of Physics, Professor for Computational Quantum Mechanics', email: 'Verified email at univie.ac.at - Homepage', published_content: 'density functional theoryfirst principles calculationsmany body theorycondensed matter physicsmaterials science' }

然后，我们将抓取作者发布的内容。

这是我们的代码：

 for el in soup.select("#gsc_a_b .gsc_a_t"): article = { 'title': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at").text, 'link': "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select_one(".gsc_a_at")['href'], 'authors': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at+ .gs_gray").text, 'publication': el.select_one(".gs_gray+ .gs_gray").text } articles.append(article) for i in range(len(articles)): articles[i] = {k: v for k, v in articles[i].items() if v and v != ""}

结果应该是这样的：

 [ { title: 'Efficient iterative schemes for ab initio total-energy calculations using a plane-wave basis set', link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ&citation_for_view=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ:a3BOlSfXSfwC', authors: 'G Kresse, J Furthmüller', publication: 'Physical review B 54 (16), 11169, 1996' }, { title: 'From ultrasoft pseudopotentials to the projector augmented-wave method', link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ&citation_for_view=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ:F9fV5C73w3QC', authors: 'G Kresse, D Joubert', publication: 'Physical review b 59 (3), 1758, 1999' },

现在，我们将抓取 Google Scholar Author profile Cited By 结果，其中我们将涵盖自 2017 年以来的引文、h-index 和 i10-index。

这是代码：

 cited_by = {} cited_by['table'] = [] cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][0]['citations'] = {} cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][1]['h_index'] = {} cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][2]['i_index'] = {} cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text Here are the results: [ { citations: { all: '345869', since_2017: '168297' } }, { h_index: { all: '132', since_2017: '82' } }, { i_index: { all: '367', since_2017: '276' } } ]

这是抓取作者完整个人资料的完整代码：

 import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup def getAuthorProfileData(): try: url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=cOsxSDEAAAAJ" headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/104.0.0.0 Safari/537.36" } response = requests.get(url, headers=headers) soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser') author_results = {} articles = [] author_results['name'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_in").text author_results['position'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_inw+ .gsc_prf_il").text author_results['email'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_ivh").text author_results['departments'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_int").text for el in soup.select("#gsc_a_b .gsc_a_t"): article = { 'title': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at").text, 'link': "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select_one(".gsc_a_at")['href'], 'authors': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at+ .gs_gray").text, 'publication': el.select_one(".gs_gray+ .gs_gray").text } articles.append(article) for i in range(len(articles)): articles[i] = {k: v for k, v in articles[i].items() if v and v != ""} cited_by = {} cited_by['table'] = [] cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][0]['citations'] = {} cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][1]['h_index'] = {} cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'].append({}) cited_by['table'][2]['i_index'] = {} cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text print(author_results) print(articles) print(cited_by['table']) except Exception as e: print(e) getAuthorProfileData()

使用谷歌学术 API

对于经常被谷歌封锁的开发者来说，抓取谷歌学术搜索可能很困难。 此外，必须根据不断变化的 HTML 结构相应地维护爬虫。

假设为您提供了一种直接有效的解决方案来抓取 Google 学术搜索结果，这不是一个很好的选择吗？

是的，你没听错！ 我们的Google Scholar API允许企业使用我们强大的 API 基础架构从 Googe Scholar 大规模抓取教育内容，该基础架构由超过 1000 万个住宅代理提供支持。

我们还在首次注册时提供 100 个免费请求。

在我们的网站上注册后，您将获得一个 API 密钥。 在下面的代码中嵌入此 API 密钥，您将能够以更快的速度抓取 Google 学术搜索结果。

 import requests payload = {'api_key': 'APIKEY', 'q':'quantum+physics'} resp = requests.get('https://api.serpdog.io/scholar', params=payload) print (resp.text)

结论：

本教程教我们使用 Python 抓取 Google Scholar。 如果我遗漏了什么或者您需要澄清什么，请随时给我发消息。 在Twitter上关注我 感谢阅读！

其他资源

一旦暂停，serpdogapi 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，serpdogapi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，serpdogapi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 serpdogapi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 serpdogapi 的操作：

使 serpdogapi 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 serpdogapi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Google Scholar data can be a great choice for businesses that specifically want to access quality researched based content available on the internet.

In this tutorial, we will learn to scrape Google Scholar Results using Python and libraries requests and BeautifulSoup.

Why Scrape Google Scholar?

Scraping Google Scholar can provide you with various benefits:

Content Access — Scraping Google Scholar allows you to get access to research-based content published by highly experienced scientists around the world.

Analyzing Trends — Developers can analyze trends in the scholarly literature by extracting information like articles title, abstracts, and citation counts from Google Scholar Results

Let’s start scraping Google Scholar using Python

In this section, we are going to prepare a basic script using Python to scrape Google Scholar Results, but let us first complete the requirements of this project.

Set-Up

If you have not already installed Python on your device, please consider these videos:

  1. How to install Python on Windows?

  2. How to install Python on MacOS?

Or you can directly install Python from their official website.

Requirements:

To scrape Google Scholar Results, we will be using these two Python libraries:

You can run the below commands in your project terminal to install the libraries.

pip install requests
pip install beautifulsoup4

Scraping Google Scholar Organic Results

In this section, we are going to scrape the organic results from Google Scholar.

Open this link on your desktop. We will scrape the title, link, id, displayed link, snippet, and site links from those results.

Here is the complete code to scrape the Google Organic Scholar Results:

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def getScholarData():
    try:
        url = "https://www.google.com/scholar?q=Quantum+Physics&hl=en"
        headers = {
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_11_3) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36"
        }
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

        scholar_results = []

        for el in soup.select(".gs_ri"):
            scholar_results.append({
                "title": el.select(".gs_rt")[0].text,
                "title_link": el.select(".gs_rt a")[0]["href"],
                "id": el.select(".gs_rt a")[0]["id"],
                "displayed_link": el.select(".gs_a")[0].text,
                "snippet": el.select(".gs_rs")[0].text.replace("\n", ""),
                "cited_by_count": el.select(".gs_nph+ a")[0].text,
                "cited_link": "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select(".gs_nph+ a")[0]["href"],
                "versions_count": el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0].text,
                "versions_link": "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0]["href"] if el.select("a~ a+ .gs_nph")[0].text else "",
            })

        for i in range(len(scholar_results)):
            scholar_results[i] = {key: value for key, value in scholar_results[i].items() if value != "" and value is not None}

        print(scholar_results)

    except Exception as e:
        print(e)

getScholarData()

You can use this CSS Selectors Gadget to find the tags for the respective elements you want from the HTML results.

This will return the following data from the web page:

[
 {
  'title': '[BOOK][B] Quantum physics',
  'title_link': 'https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=qFtQiVmjWUEC&oi=fnd&pg=PA28&dq=Quantum+Physics&ots=tuFPLNEOmu&sig=tRYUJyK8VECC_j1Lwpbl6bjC5ag',
  'id': 'cU32d0ZoSA0J',
  'displayed_link': 'S Gasiorowicz - 2007 - books.google.com',
  'snippet': '… the Old Quantum Theory. This material appears in every textbook on modern physics in one … the creation of quantum mechanics, and it highlights the differences between classical and …',
  'cited_by_count': 'Cited by 1400',
  'cited_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cites=957129572685860209&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en',
  'versions_count': 'All 10 versions',
  'versions_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cluster=957129572685860209&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5'
 },
 {
  'title': '[BOOK][B] Quantum physics',
  'title_link': 'https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=hBlZu4M51IMC&oi=fnd&pg=PA1&dq=Quantum+Physics&ots=uEsu7Kytk-&sig=54fJyjI177Zm2gC7HZcgM_iCEJU',
  'id': 'ENaNHF5MoQsJ',
  'displayed_link': 'M Le Bellac - 2011 - books.google.com',
  'snippet': '… allow the fundamentals of quantum mechanics to be tested … in quantum physics. It will also allow us to give a small sample of the current ideas on the notion of measurement in quantum …',
  'cited_by_count': 'Cited by 211',
  'cited_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cites=838034972757317136&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en',
  'versions_count': 'All 4 versions',
  'versions_link': 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?cluster=838034972757317136&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5'
 },
 ....

Scraping Google Scholar Cite Results

Then, we will use the IDs we got from scraping organic results to scrape the cite results.

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
def getData():
    try:
        url = "https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com&output=cite"
        headers = {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/104.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"}
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, "html.parser")
        cite_results = []
        for el in soup.select("#gs_citt tr"):
            cite_results.append({
                "title": el.select_one(".gs_cith").text.strip(),
                "snippet": el.select_one(".gs_citr").text.strip()
            })
        links = []
        for el in soup.select("#gs_citi .gs_citi"):
            links.append({
                "name": el.text.strip(),
                "link": el.get("href")
            })
        print(cite_results)
        print(links)
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)
getData()

If you take a look at the URL, after the info we have used an ID of the first organic result we got from the above section.

Our result should look like this:

  [
    {
        'title': 'MLA', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, Stephen. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons, 2007.'
    },
    {
        'title': 'APA', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, S. (2007). Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons.'
    },
    {
        'title': 'Chicago', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, Stephen. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons, 2007.'
    },
    {
        'title': 'Harvard', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz, S., 2007. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons.'
    },
    {
        'title': 'Vancouver', 'snippet': 'Gasiorowicz S. Quantum physics. John Wiley & Sons; 2007 Jan 29.'
    }
  ]
  [
    {
        'name': 'BibTeX',
        'link': 'https: //scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.bib?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=4&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en'
    },
    {
        'name': 'EndNote',
        'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.enw?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=3&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' 
    },
    {
        'name': 'RefMan',
        'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.ris?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=2&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' 
    },
    { 
        'name': 'RefWorks',
        'link': 'https://scholar.googleusercontent.com/scholar.rfw?q=info:cU32d0ZoSA0J:scholar.google.com/&output=citation&scisdr=Cm3wRhgwGAA:AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DtNdVceWMhOLx32wQFKpqxA&scisig=AGlGAw8AAAAAZFe6DjCj95cYqQaVYG2_H2xJMDY&scisf=1&ct=citation&cd=-1&hl=en' 
    }
   ]

Scraping Google Scholar Authors

Now, let’s scrape the profiles of the authors who have published quality content on Quantum Physics.

Here is our code:

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def getScholarProfiles():
    try:
        url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&view_op=search_authors&mauthors=Quantum+Physics"
        headers = {
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.54 Safari/537.36"
        }
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')

        scholar_profiles = []
        for el in soup.select('.gsc_1usr'):
            profile = {
                'name': el.select_one('.gs_ai_name').get_text(),
                'name_link': 'https://scholar.google.com' + el.select_one('.gs_ai_name a')['href'],
                'position': el.select_one('.gs_ai_aff').get_text(),
                'email': el.select_one('.gs_ai_eml').get_text(),
                'departments': el.select_one('.gs_ai_int').get_text(),
                'cited_by_count': el.select_one('.gs_ai_cby').get_text().split(' ')[2]
            }
            scholar_profiles.append({k: v for k, v in profile.items() if v})

        print(scholar_profiles)
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)

getScholarProfiles()

Our results should look like this:

[
  {
    name: 'Georg Kresse',
    name_link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ',
    position: 'University of Vienna, Faculty of Physics, Professor for Computational Quantum Mechanics',
    email: 'Verified email at univie.ac.at',
    departments: 'density functional theory first principles calculations many body theory condensed matter physics materials science ',
    cited_by_count: '345869'
  },
  {
    name: 'Manuel Proissl',
    name_link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=ikHSFIkAAAAJ',
    position: 'Data Science Leader, Quantum Computing Technologist, Physicist',
    email: 'Verified email at accenture.com',
    departments: 'quantum computing machine learning deep learning causal inference ',
    cited_by_count: '82357'
  },
    ....

Scraping Google Scholar Author Profile

Let us not create a scraper to extract data from Google Author Profile.
First, we will extract some main details about the author then we will move to its published content part.

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def getAuthorProfileData():
    try:
        url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ"
        headers = {
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_13_5) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36"
        }
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        print(response.status_code)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
        author_results = {}
        author_results['name'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_in").get_text()
        author_results['position'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_inw+ .gsc_prf_il").text
        author_results['email'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_ivh").text
        author_results['published_content'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_int").text
        print(author_results)
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)

getAuthorProfileData()

Our result should look like this:

{
  name: 'Georg Kresse',
  position: 'University of Vienna, Faculty of Physics, Professor for Computational Quantum Mechanics',
  email: 'Verified email at univie.ac.at - Homepage',
  published_content: 'density functional theoryfirst principles calculationsmany body theorycondensed matter physicsmaterials science'
}

Then, we will scrape the content published by the author.

Here is our code:

for el in soup.select("#gsc_a_b .gsc_a_t"):
            article = {
                'title': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at").text,
                'link': "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select_one(".gsc_a_at")['href'],
                'authors': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at+ .gs_gray").text,
                'publication': el.select_one(".gs_gray+ .gs_gray").text
            }
            articles.append(article)
        for i in range(len(articles)):
            articles[i] = {k: v for k, v in articles[i].items() if v and v != ""}

And the results should look like this:

[
  {
    title: 'Efficient iterative schemes for ab initio total-energy calculations using a plane-wave basis set',
    link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ&citation_for_view=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ:a3BOlSfXSfwC',
    authors: 'G Kresse, J Furthmüller',
    publication: 'Physical review B 54 (16), 11169, 1996'
  },
  {
    title: 'From ultrasoft pseudopotentials to the projector augmented-wave method',
    link: 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ&citation_for_view=Pn8ouvAAAAAJ:F9fV5C73w3QC',
    authors: 'G Kresse, D Joubert',
    publication: 'Physical review b 59 (3), 1758, 1999'
  },

Now, we will scrape the Google Scholar Author profile Cited By results in which we will cover citation, h-index, and the i10-index since 2017.

Here is the code:

    cited_by = {}
            cited_by['table'] = []
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text

Here are the results:

    [
      { citations: { all: '345869', since_2017: '168297' } },
      { h_index: { all: '132', since_2017: '82' } },
      { i_index: { all: '367', since_2017: '276' } }
    ]

Here is the complete code to scrape the complete profile of an Author:

    import requests
    from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

    def getAuthorProfileData():
        try:
            url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=cOsxSDEAAAAJ"
            headers = {
                "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/104.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
            }
            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
            author_results = {}
            articles = []
            author_results['name'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_in").text
            author_results['position'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_inw+ .gsc_prf_il").text
            author_results['email'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_ivh").text
            author_results['departments'] = soup.select_one("#gsc_prf_int").text
            for el in soup.select("#gsc_a_b .gsc_a_t"):
                article = {
                    'title': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at").text,
                    'link': "https://scholar.google.com" + el.select_one(".gsc_a_at")['href'],
                    'authors': el.select_one(".gsc_a_at+ .gs_gray").text,
                    'publication': el.select_one(".gs_gray+ .gs_gray").text
                }
                articles.append(article)
            for i in range(len(articles)):
                articles[i] = {k: v for k, v in articles[i].items() if v and v != ""}
            cited_by = {}
            cited_by['table'] = []
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][0]['citations']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(1) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][1]['h_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr:nth-child(2) .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'].append({})
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index'] = {}
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['all'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_sc1+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            cited_by['table'][2]['i_index']['since_2017'] = soup.select_one("tr~ tr+ tr .gsc_rsb_std+ .gsc_rsb_std").text
            print(author_results)
            print(articles)
            print(cited_by['table'])
        except Exception as e:
            print(e)

    getAuthorProfileData()

Using Google Scholar API

Scraping Google Scholar can be difficult for a developer with frequent blockage from Google. Also, one has to maintain the scraper accordingly with the changing HTML structure.

Suppose you were provided with a straightforward and efficient solution to scrape Google Scholar Results, wouldn’t that be an excellent choice?

Yes, you heard right! Our Google Scholar API allows businesses to scrape educational content from Googe Scholar at scale using our powerful API infrastructure which is powered by a massive pool of 10M+ residential proxies.

We also offer 100 free requests on the first sign-up.

After getting registered on our website, you will get an API Key. Embed this API Key in the below code, you will be able to scrape Google Scholar Results at a much faster speed.

  import requests
    payload = {'api_key': 'APIKEY', 'q':'quantum+physics'}
    resp = requests.get('https://api.serpdog.io/scholar', params=payload)
    print (resp.text)

Conclusion:

This tutorial taught us to scrape Google Scholar using Python. Feel free to message me if I missed something or if anything you need clarification on. Follow me on Twitter Thanks for reading!

Additional Resources

Akbar Julian Khatibi - May 18

item.title: 节点抽象和多态存储库 | Node Abstract and Polymorphic Repository
item.description:
乔苏·马丁内斯

发表于 6 月 3 日

我在ClimatePartner的开始还应该开始使用 JS 和 TS 相关技术堆栈开发 Web 应用程序。 我的第一个任务是将一个小型后端 NestJS 应用程序与 MongoDB 数据库连接起来。 我们对我们的应用程序有很大的计划； 我们希望它能够对我们团队控制下的多个领域进行建模，因此我们很快意识到我们的领域模型将变得越来越复杂，而且速度会很快。 此外，我们发现这也是我们组织内想要使用相同类型解决方案的其他团队的现实。

综合考虑，我们决定合并一个实现存储库模式的数据库访问实用程序最适合实现我们的兴趣。 此外，我们希望它具有抽象性，以便我们可以重用公共数据库访问逻辑并为我们的每个域对象创建自定义存储库。 然后，每个自定义存储库都可以通过实现与手头的域对象相关的新数据库操作来扩展通用逻辑。 最后，我们需要它来处理多态域模型。

所以我们开始搜索，发现了几个显然符合我们要求的库。 我们的候选者中最好的是 Mongoose、TypeORM 和 Typegoose。 猫鼬是

来自构建为此目的使用 Spring Data 的应用程序，我搜索了一个实现存储库模式的数据库持久性实用程序

原文:

Josu Martinez

Posted on Jun 3

My start at ClimatePartner also supposed starting to develop web applications with a JS and TS-related tech stack. My very first assignment was connecting a small backend NestJS app with a MongoDB database. We had big plans for our app; we wanted it to model several domains that were under the control of our team, so we soon realised that our domain model was going to grow in complexity, and quickly. Moreover, we saw that this was also the reality in other teams within our organisation that wanted to use the same kind of solution.

All considered, we decided that incorporating a database access utility that implements the Repository pattern was the best fit to achieve our interests. Moreover, we wanted it to be of an abstract nature so that we could reuse common database access logic and create custom repositories for each of our domain objects. Each custom repository could then extend the common logic by implementing new database operations pertaining to the domain object at hand. Finally, we needed it to handle polymorphic domain models.

So we started our search, and discovered several libraries that apparently fitted our requirements. The best of our candidates were Mongoose, TypeORM, and Typegoose. Mongoose is

Coming from building apps that use Spring Data for such a purpose, I searched for a database persistence utility that implements the Repository Pattern

Brian Bethencourt - Jun 2

K M Rejowan Ahmmed - Jun 2

item.title: 关于墨尔本家具存放您需要知道的一切 | Everything You Need To Know About Furniture Storage in Melbourne
item.description:
您住在墨尔本，需要存放家具吗？ 好吧，有许多解决方案，既有短期的也有长期的。 要确定最合适的存储选择，必须首先熟悉可用的各种类型。

为什么有人可能需要存放家具？

存在许多可能需要存储家具的情况。 这些包括家庭装修、搬迁或当前家中空间不足。 无论基本原理如何，都必须在家具入库前相应地包装和准备家具，以确保物品在离开家期间的持续安全和保障。

服务式存储

一种选择是服务式存储，它可以在存储家具时节省您的时间和精力。 这种类型的存储涉及一家公司将集装箱直接送到您家门口。 然后，您可以根据需要花费尽可能多的时间。

在容器被收集并带走存放之前，他们会协助收拾您的物品。 服务式仓储的优势在于您不必担心自己运输物品，因为这一切都由提供服务的公司负责。

不同尺寸

在墨尔本选择家具储物单元时，尺寸是一个重要的考虑因素。 以下是典型单元尺寸的细目分类，以及每个单元可以容纳多少间卧室的家具：

  • 选择单元尺寸时，重要的是要考虑需要存放的家具数量。 一个全面的经验法则是，一间卧室的家具可以装进一个 10 立方米的单元。 请记住，这可能会有所不同，具体取决于需要存储的具体物品和您使用的设施。

    重要注意事项

    在墨尔本选择家具存储供应商时，考虑每个设施提供的安全措施至关重要。 为确保您的物品在储存或运输过程中的安全，请寻找 24/7 闭路电视监控摄像头和单独报警装置等功能。
    此外，请考虑供应商可能提供的任何额外服务，例如个性化的取件和送货或长期合同的折扣，以获得物有所值的服务。 对这些因素进行全面评估将帮助您在墨尔本选择信誉良好且安全的家具存储选项。

    结论

    总而言之，在墨尔本寻找最佳家具存储服务解决方案时有很多选择——从短期和长期解决方案到服务容器和各种尺寸的单元——所以在做出任何决定之前一定要进行研究！



原文:


Are you based in Melbourne and need to store furniture? Well, there are numerous solutions, both short and long-term in nature. To determine the most suitable storage choice, one must first acquaint themselves with the varying types available.

Why Someone May Need to Store Furniture?

There exists a multitude of circumstances that may necessitate the storage of furniture. These include home renovations, relocation, or inadequate space within one's current home. Irrespective of the underlying rationale, it is compulsory to pack and prepare the furniture accordingly prior to its placement into storage to guarantee the continued safety and security of the items during their absence from the home.

Serviced Storage

One option is serviced storage, which can save you time and effort when storing furniture. This type of storage involves a company delivering a container directly to your door. You can then take as much time as you need.

They'll assist in packing up your belongings before the container is collected and taken away for storage. The advantage of serviced storage is that you don't have to worry about transporting your items yourself, as this is all taken care of by the company providing the service.

Different Sizes

When choosing a furniture storage unit in Melbourne, the size is an important consideration. Here is a breakdown of the typical unit sizes and how many bedrooms worth of furniture can fit in each one:

  • 10m³: Ideal for storing items from a single bedroom.
  • 20m³: Can accommodate furniture from two bedrooms.
  • 30m³: Suitable for storing items from three bedrooms.
  • 40m³: Can hold furniture from up to four bedrooms.
  • 50m³: A good option for storing belongings from five bedrooms.
  • 100m³: Can accommodate furniture from up to eight bedrooms.
  • 150m³: Can hold furniture from up to ten bedrooms.
  • 200m³: Suitable for storing items from thirteen bedrooms or more.

When selecting your unit size, it's important to consider the amount of furniture that needs to be stored. An all-around rule of thumb is that one bedroom's worth of furniture can fit into a 10m³ unit. Keep in mind that this may vary depending on the specific items that need to be stored and the facility you're working with.

Important Considerations

When selecting a furniture storage provider in Melbourne, it's crucial to take into account the security measures each facility offers. To ensure the safety of your items during storage or transport, look for features like 24/7 CCTV surveillance cameras and individually alarmed units.
Additionally, consider any extra services that the provider may offer, such as personalized pick-up and delivery or discounts for long-term contracts, to get the best value for your money. A thorough evaluation of these factors will help you select a reputable and secure furniture storage option in Melbourne.

Conclusion

In summary, there are plenty of options available when it comes to finding best furniture storage service solutions in Melbourne - from short & long-term solutions through to serviced containers and various sizes of units - so make sure you research before making any decisions!
item.author: zafoxinub

item.title: 使用 Node.JS 管理团队（第 2 部分） | MANAGING A TEAM WITH NODE.JS (PART 2)
item.description:
如果您错过了本文的第一部分，请阅读此处以赶上进度

在上一篇文章中，我们能够

  • 创建团队
  • 发送团队邀请，
  • 接受团队邀请

在本文中，我们将讨论

  • 拒绝邀请
  • 离开团队
  • 从团队中删除成员
  • 管理团队中的角色

迅速地，

  • 让我们创建两个新用户，
  • 将一个用户的邀请发送给另一个用户，
  • 然后我们从那里继续

有效载荷

// user 1 { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "password": "test1234" } // user 2 { "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "password": "test1234" }

步骤1

让我们把 john doe 的邀请发给 jane doe

有效载荷：

 { "email": "janedoe@gmail.com" }

回复：

 { "status": "success", "data": "invitation sent to janedoe@gmail.com" }

第2步

让我们看一下 john 和 jane 的用户对象

// john "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 1, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 5, "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846" } }

在 john 的对象中，team 数组包含一个只有一名成员的团队，即 john，他是管理员

{ "status": "success", "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpZCI6IjY0N2FmZDQwMTRjMmY0OGRmYzE4Yzg0OSIsImlhdCI6MTY4NTc4MjUwMiwiZXhwIjoxNjkzNTU4NTAyfQ.NABFYJzvEvOfWUwy5cBnecIrHgDflrF3QVaAsYw_rcc", "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 2, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } } }

在 jane 用户对象中，team 数组是空的，因为很明显，jane 还不是任何团队的成员，

并且邀请数组包含一个团队， nodejs team ，由 john 发送的邀请。

第 3 步

拒绝邀请

现在让我们计算一下如何拒绝约翰发出的邀请。

回到我们的团队controller file

注意：基本上这个概念是在邀请数组中找到团队的索引并使用Array.splice()方法将其删除。

 // reject team invitation exports.rejectInvitation = async (req, res) => { // find user in db const user = await User.findById(req.user.id); // find team in db // teamID will be gotted as a parameter when we run the request const team = await Team.findById(req.params.teamId); // check if team exists if (!team) { return res.status(404).json({ status: "failed", message: "team not found", }); } // if team exists, let us find the index of the team in the invitations array const teamIndex = user.invitations.indexOf(team.id); // remove team id from the invitations array user.invitations.splice(teamIndex); // save changes await user.save(); return res.status(200).json({ status: "success", message: "invitation rejected", }); };

步骤4

让我们为这个请求创建一个路由并在 post man 上运行它，注意：传递团队 ID 作为这个请求的参数

router.post( "/reject-invitation/:teamId", middleware.protect, teamController.rejectInvitation );

回复：

 { "status": "success", "message": "invitation rejected" }

为确认邀请已被拒绝，让我们再次查看 jane 的用户对象。

 "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 3, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

现在邀请数组是空的，因为我们已经成功地从邀请数组中删除了团队。

第 5 步

离开团队

为了计算这个，让我们向 jane 发送一个新邀请，然后登录到 jane 帐户接受邀请，然后我们就可以继续离开团队了。

因为你必须是一个团队的成员才能离开一个团队。

回复：

 { "status": "success", "data": "invitation sent to janedoe@gmail.com" }

回复：

 { "status": "success", "message": "invitation to nodejs team accepted" }

让我们看一下 jane 用户对象，以确保她现在是 nodejs 团队的成员

"data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" }, { "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "role": "member" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 2, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 5, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

如您所见，node.js 团队有两名成员，管理员是 john doe，jane doe 是成员。

第 6 步

让我们继续我们的控制器并计算离开团队

exports.leaveTeam = async (req, res) => { // find user in db const user = await User.findById(req.user.id); // find team in db const team = await Team.findById(req.params.teamId); // check if team exists if (!team) { return res.status(404).json({ status: "failed", message: "team not found", }); } // if team exist, check if user is a member of the team const member = team.members.find((item) => item.id === user.id); // if user is not a member, throw an error and terminate if (!member) { return res.status(400).json({ status: "failed", message: `you are not a member of ${team.name.toUpperCase()}`, }); } // if user is a member, find index of team in the team array on the user object const teamIndex = user.teams.indexOf(team.id); // delete team from team array user.teams.splice(teamIndex); // remove user from member array on the team const newMembers = team.members.filter((item) => item.id !== member.id); team.members = newMembers; // save changes await team.save(); await user.save(); return res.status(200).json({ status: "success", message: `successfully exited ${team.name}`, }); };

第 7 步

让我们为这个请求创建一个路由并在邮递员上运行它

router.post( "/leave-team/:teamId", middleware.protect, teamController.leaveTeam );

回复：

 { "status": "success", "message": "successfully exited nodejs team" }

为确认它是否成功，让我们再次查看 jane 用户对象，

 "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 6, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

正如预期的那样，teams 数组为空，这意味着请求成功。

第 8 步

从团队中删除成员

让我们重复向 jane 发送邀请并接受邀请的过程，然后重新登录 john 帐户将 jane 从团队中删除

 "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" }, { "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "role": "member" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 4, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 8, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

第 9 步

让我们为这个请求创建一个路由并在邮递员中运行它

router.post( "/remove-member", middleware.protect, teamController.removeMemberFromTeam );
 // request url http://localhost:8000/api/v1/team/remove-member?teamId=647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b&userId=647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849

回复：

 { "status": "success", "message": "jane doe successfully removed from nodejs team" }

为了确认，让我们再看看 nodejs 团队

"teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" } ],

到目前为止，我们已经能够

  • 创建团队
  • 发送团队邀请
  • 接受团队邀请
  • 拒绝团队邀请
  • 离开团队
  • 从团队中删除成员

在本文的最后一部分，我们将讨论团队中的角色管理

我希望你很兴奋。

同时，请在评论部分告诉我您对此的想法。

在这里获取我的源代码
在此处查看我的 API 文档

一旦暂停，candie_code 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，candie_code 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，candie_code 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 candie_code 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 candie_code：

降低 candie_code 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 candie_code 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

In case you missed the first part of this article, read here to catch up

In the last article, we were able to

  • Create a team
  • Send team invitation,
  • Accept team invitation

In this article, we will be talking about

  • Rejecting Invitation
  • Leaving a team
  • Removing member from a team
  • Managing roles in a team

Quickly,

  • let us create two new users,
  • Send invitation from one user to another,
  • then we continue from there

PAYLOAD

// user 1
{ "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "password": "test1234"
}
// user 2
{ "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "password": "test1234"
}

STEP 1

Let us send invitation from john doe to jane doe

PAYLOAD:

{ "email": "janedoe@gmail.com"
}

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "data": "invitation sent to janedoe@gmail.com"
}

STEP 2

Let us take a look at the user object for john and jane

// john "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 1, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 5, "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846" } }

Inside john's object, the team array contains a team with only one member, which is john, and he is the admin

{ "status": "success", "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpZCI6IjY0N2FmZDQwMTRjMmY0OGRmYzE4Yzg0OSIsImlhdCI6MTY4NTc4MjUwMiwiZXhwIjoxNjkzNTU4NTAyfQ.NABFYJzvEvOfWUwy5cBnecIrHgDflrF3QVaAsYw_rcc", "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 2, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }
}

In the jane user object, the team array is empty, because obviously, jane is not a member of any team yet,

And the invitation array contains one team, nodejs team, the invitation that was sent by john.

STEP 3

REJECTING INVITATION

Now let us compute how to reject the invitation sent by john.

Back to our team controller file

NOTE: Basically the concept is finding the index of the team in the invitation array and deleting it with the Array.splice() method.

// reject team invitation
exports.rejectInvitation = async (req, res) => { // find user in db const user = await User.findById(req.user.id); // find team in db // teamID will be gotted as a parameter when we run the request const team = await Team.findById(req.params.teamId); // check if team exists if (!team) { return res.status(404).json({ status: "failed", message: "team not found", }); } // if team exists, let us find the index of the team in the invitations array const teamIndex = user.invitations.indexOf(team.id); // remove team id from the invitations array user.invitations.splice(teamIndex); // save changes await user.save(); return res.status(200).json({ status: "success", message: "invitation rejected", });
};

STEP 4

Let us create a route for this request and run it on post man, NOTE: Pass the team ID as a parameter for this request

router.post( "/reject-invitation/:teamId", middleware.protect, teamController.rejectInvitation
);

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "message": "invitation rejected"
}

To confirm the invitation was rejected, let us take a look at the user object for jane again.

"data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 3, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

Now the invitation array is empty, because we have successfully deleted the team from the invitation array.

STEP 5

LEAVING A TEAM

To compute this, let us send a new invitation to jane, then login to jane account to accept the invitation, then we can proceed to leave a team.

Because you have to be a member of a team to leave a team.

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "data": "invitation sent to janedoe@gmail.com"
}

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "message": "invitation to nodejs team accepted"
}

Let us take a look at jane user object to be sure she's now a member of nodejs team

"data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" }, { "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "role": "member" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 2, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 5, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

As you can see, the node.js team has two members, the admin which is john doe, and a jane doe, a member.

STEP 6

Let us proceed to our controller and compute leaving a team

exports.leaveTeam = async (req, res) => { // find user in db const user = await User.findById(req.user.id); // find team in db const team = await Team.findById(req.params.teamId); // check if team exists if (!team) { return res.status(404).json({ status: "failed", message: "team not found", }); } // if team exist, check if user is a member of the team const member = team.members.find((item) => item.id === user.id); // if user is not a member, throw an error and terminate if (!member) { return res.status(400).json({ status: "failed", message: `you are not a member of ${team.name.toUpperCase()}`, }); } // if user is a member, find index of team in the team array on the user object const teamIndex = user.teams.indexOf(team.id); // delete team from team array user.teams.splice(teamIndex); // remove user from member array on the team const newMembers = team.members.filter((item) => item.id !== member.id); team.members = newMembers; // save changes await team.save(); await user.save(); return res.status(200).json({ status: "success", message: `successfully exited ${team.name}`, });
};

STEP 7

Let us create a route for this request and run it on postman

router.post( "/leave-team/:teamId", middleware.protect, teamController.leaveTeam
);

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "message": "successfully exited nodejs team"
}

To confirm it was successful, let us take a look at the jane user object once again,

 "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 6, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

As expected, the teams array is empty, which means the request was successful.

STEP 8

REMOVING MEMBER FROM A TEAM

Let us repeat the process of sending an invitation to jane and accepting the invitation, then logging in back to john account to remove jane from the team

 "data": { "user": { "_id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" }, { "name": "jane doe", "email": "janedoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849", "role": "member" } ], "userId": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:51:44.822Z", "__v": 4, "id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b" } ], "invitations": [], "createdAt": "2023-06-03T08:41:06.544Z", "__v": 8, "id": "647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849" } }

Jane is already a member of the nodejs team, now let us log back in to john's account to compute removing jane from the nodejs team.

exports.removeMemberFromTeam = async (req, res) => { // extract userId and teamId from the request query const { userId, teamId } = req.query; // find team in db const team = await Team.findById(teamId); const user = await User.findById(userId); // check if team exist if (!team) { return res.status(404).json({ status: "failed", message: "team not found", }); } // if team exist, check if user is a member of the team const member = team.members.find((item) => item.id === userId); // if user is not a member, throw an error and terminate if (!member) { return res.status(400).json({ status: "failed", message: `you are not a member of ${team.name.toUpperCase()}`, }); } // if user is a member, filter user out of the team const newTeamMember = team.members.filter((item) => item.id !== userId); team.members = newTeamMember; // remove teamId from teams array on user object const teamIndex = user.teams.indexOf(team.id); user.teams.splice(teamIndex); // save changes await team.save(); await user.save(); return res.status(200).json({ status: "success", message: `${user.name} successfully removed from ${team.name}`, });
};

STEP 9

Let us create a route for this request and run it in postman

router.post( "/remove-member", middleware.protect, teamController.removeMemberFromTeam
);

// request url
http://localhost:8000/api/v1/team/remove-member?teamId=647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b&userId=647afd4014c2f48dfc18c849

RESPONSE:

{ "status": "success", "message": "jane doe successfully removed from nodejs team"
}

To confirm, let us take a look at the nodejs team again

"teams": [ { "_id": "647aff306dcdb9d9546c7b1b", "name": "nodejs team", "members": [ { "name": "john doe", "email": "johndoe@gmail.com", "id": "647afd1914c2f48dfc18c846", "role": "admin" } ],

Nodejs team only contains john, who is the admin, meaning our logic works too.

So far we have been able to

  • Create a team
  • Send team invitation
  • Accept team invitation
  • Reject team invitation
  • Leave team
  • Remove member from team

In the final part of this article, we will be talking about ROLE MANAGEMENT IN A TEAM

I hope you are excited.

Meanwhile Let me know your thoughts on this in the comments section.

Get my source code here
View my API documentation here

Akbar Julian Khatibi - May 18

item.title: 创造可转移的知识 | Creating Transferrable Knowledge
item.description:
海尼克·哈尔温

发表于 6 月 3 日

大家好 ！
我是一名全栈开发人员，通常使用 python django 或 javascript 框架，例如 react 或 next。 最近我不得不使用 Kotlin Android 为我的大学创建一个项目。

通常，我处理新项目的方式是，投入时间，学习编程语言，学习最佳实践，观看教程，阅读 DEV 上的帖子 ;) 等等。 但是在这个项目中，由于时间限制，我不得不直接投入其中，我想我可能会遇到问题或者那将是一场灾难。 但相反，它进行得很顺利，我意识到我拥有可转移的知识。

我所说的可转移知识的意思是跨所有语言和框架识别和使用编程概念的能力。 因为在每种语言的花里胡哨之下，它们几乎都遵循相同的编程范例和概念。

这让我想到下一点，如何创造可转移的知识。

是的，学习编程概念！ 从数据类型、数据结构、条件语句、循环语句等小概念到异常处理等重要概念。此外，您还可以学习函数式编程概念，例如柯里化、纯函数等。面向对象编程概念也是如此.

现在您已经了解了这些概念。 您可以选择一种语言来处理您的副业。 在这里，您可以根据您所学的概念分解逻辑，然后您可以参考语法的文档或教程，通过您的应用程序逻辑进行编码，几个小时后，您将完成语法如果这是第二天性。

恭喜 ！ 您现在已经开始创建您的可转移知识。

你在你的职业生涯中经历过并使用过这个吗？ 如果你有，请在评论中告诉我。

原文:

Heinek Halwin

Posted on Jun 3

Hi everyone !
I am a Full stack developer usually working with python django or javascript frameworks such as react or next. And recently I had to work with Kotlin Android to create a project for my university.

Usually, the way I work with a new project is, I put my hours in, I learn the programming language, go through the best practices, watch tutorials, read posts here on DEV ;) and so on. But in this project, I had to dive right in because of time constraints and I imagined I might bump into problems or that it would be a disaster. But instead it went pretty well and I realised I had transferrable knowledge.

What i mean by transferrable knowledge is the ability to identify and use programming concepts across all languages and frameworks. Because underneath all the bells and whistles of each language, they all follow mostly the same programming paradigms and concepts.

Which brings me to next point, how to create transferrable knowledge.

*Firstly and lastly, Learn programming concepts. *

Yes, learn programming concepts! From concepts small as datatypes, data structures, conditional statements, looping statements to concepts vital such as exception handling etc. Also for bonus points, you can learn about Functional programming concepts such as currying, pure functions etc. Same goes for object oriented programming concepts.

Now that you have learned the concepts. You can choose a language to work on your side hustle. Here, you can break down the logic in terms of the concepts you have learned, then you can refer the documentation or tutorials for the syntax to code through the logic of your application and in a few hours, you would be working through the syntax as if it were second nature.

Congrats ! You have now started with creating your transferrable knowledge.

Have you experienced and used this in your career ? Let me know in the comments if you have.

Suraj Vishwakarma - May 12

shrey vijayvargiya - May 21

item.title: Java 中的继承：你需要知道的一切 | Inheritance in Java: Everything You Need to Know
item.description:
发表于 6 月 3 日

继承是 Java 中最强大的特性之一。 它允许您创建继承现有类的属性和方法的新类。 这可以在开发新代码时节省您的时间和精力，因为您可以重用现有类中的代码。

因为我在大学学习时使用这种编程语言，所以我发现它是一个非常有用的工具。 它让我对 OOP 有了更多的了解。

关于继承，我最喜欢的一件事是它允许我根据对象来考虑我的代码。 这使我更容易理解和维护我的代码。

例如，我最近不得不开发一个管理车队的应用程序。 我创建了一个 Vehicle 类，然后为车队中的每种类型的车辆创建了 Vehicle 的子类。 这使得编写应用程序的不同功能变得非常容易，例如启动和停止车辆以及跟踪它们的位置。

如果您是 Java 的新手，我鼓励您学习继承。 这是一个强大的功能，可以帮助您编写更好的代码。

以下是有效使用继承的一些技巧：

尽可能使用单一继承。 除非绝对必要，否则避免使用多重继承。 使用继承来重用代码，而不是创建新功能。 保持继承层次结构浅。 使用接口来解耦对象。 使用抽象类来提供接口的部分实现。

我希望这篇文章能让您更好地理解 Java 中的继承。 如果您有任何问题，请随时问我。

原文:

E A Yoga Wilanda

Posted on Jun 3

Inheritance in Java: A Personal Perspective
Inheritance is one of the most powerful features in Java. It allows you to create new classes that inherit the properties and methods of existing classes. This can save you time and effort when developing new code, as you can reuse the code from existing classes.

Since i am using this programming language as for my study in university, and I've found it to be a very helpful tool. It has allowed me to make me understands more about OOP's.

One of the things I like most about inheritance is that it allows me to think about my code in terms of objects. This makes it easier for me to understand and maintain my code.

For example, I recently had to develop an application that managed a fleet of vehicles. I created a Vehicle class, and then I created subclasses of Vehicle for each type of vehicle in the fleet. This made it very easy to code the different features of the application, such as starting and stopping the vehicles, and tracking their location.

If you're new to Java, I encourage you to learn about inheritance. It's a powerful feature that can help you to write better code.

Here are a few tips for using inheritance effectively:

Use single inheritance whenever possible. Avoid using multiple inheritance unless it is absolutely necessary. Use inheritance to reuse code, not to create new functionality. Keep inheritance hierarchies shallow. Use interfaces to decouple objects. Use abstract classes to provide partial implementations of interfaces.

I hope this article has given you a better understanding of inheritance in Java. If you have any questions, please feel free to ask me.

Codewithrandom Blogs - Jun 3

Codewithrandom Blogs - Jun 3

item.title: 新的 JavaScript 数组方法：开发人员指南 | New JavaScript Array Methods: A Guide for Developers
item.description:
维沙纳亚克

发表于 6 月 3 日

近年来，JavaScript 语言为 Array 对象添加了许多新方法，使得使用数组变得更加容易。

以下是一些新的 JavaScript 数组方法，具有更详细的描述：

  • 这对于诸如按时间倒序对项目列表进行排序或按字母顺序倒序显示项目列表等任务很有用。

  • 这对于按升序或降序对项目列表进行排序或按字母顺序显示项目列表等任务很有用。

  • 这对于诸如从列表中删除项目的一部分或将项目的新部分插入列表中的任务很有用。

  • 这对于向列表添加新项目或更新列表中的现有项目等任务很有用。

这些新方法可用于使您的 JavaScript 代码更加简洁和高效。 例如，以下代码使用toReversed()方法来反转数组的顺序：

 const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const reversedArray = array.toReversed(); console.log(reversedArray); // [5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

以下代码使用toSorted()方法对数组进行排序：

 const array = [5, 3, 1, 2, 4]; const sortedArray = array.toSorted(); console.log(sortedArray); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

以下代码使用toSpliced()方法拼接出数组的一部分：

 const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const splicedArray = array.toSpliced(2, 2); console.log(splicedArray); // [1, 2]

以下代码使用with()方法向数组添加新元素：

 const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const newArray = array.with(6, "6"); console.log(newArray); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

这些只是如何使用新的 JavaScript 数组方法的几个示例。 有关详细信息，请参阅 JavaScript 文档。

我希望本文的这个更具描述性的版本对您有所帮助。 如果您有任何其他问题，请告诉我。

原文:

Vishal Nayak

Posted on Jun 3

JavaScript arrays are a powerful tool for storing and manipulating data. In recent years, the JavaScript language has added a number of new methods to the Array object, making it even easier to work with arrays.

Here are some of the new JavaScript array methods, in more descriptive detail:

  • toReversed(): This method clones an array and then reverses its order. This can be useful for tasks such as sorting a list of items in reverse chronological order or displaying a list of items in reverse alphabetical order.

  • toSorted(): This method clones an array and then sorts it. This can be useful for tasks such as sorting a list of items in ascending or descending order or displaying a list of items in alphabetical order.

  • toSpliced(): This method clones an array and then splices out a section of it. This can be useful for tasks such as removing a section of items from a list or inserting a new section of items into a list.

  • with(): This method clones an array and then adds a new element to it. This can be useful for tasks such as adding a new item to a list or updating an existing item in a list.

These new methods can be used to make your JavaScript code more concise and efficient. For example, the following code uses the toReversed() method to reverse the order of an array:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

const reversedArray = array.toReversed();

console.log(reversedArray); // [5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

The following code uses the toSorted() method to sort an array:

const array = [5, 3, 1, 2, 4];

const sortedArray = array.toSorted();

console.log(sortedArray); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

The following code uses the toSpliced() method to splice out a section of an array:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

const splicedArray = array.toSpliced(2, 2);

console.log(splicedArray); // [1, 2]

The following code uses the with() method to add a new element to an array:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

const newArray = array.with(6, "6");

console.log(newArray); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

These are just a few examples of how the new JavaScript array methods can be used. For more information, please consult the JavaScript documentation.

I hope this more descriptive version of the article is helpful. Please let me know if you have any other questions.

Jakub Andrzejewski - May 22
